steps.title: Use the roots to write the factored form and expand
steps.content:
Since the parabola passes through $(9, 0)$ and $(-2, 0)$, these are the $x$-intercepts. With leading coefficient $a$:
$$f(x) = a(x - 9)(x + 2)$$
Expanding:
$$f(x) = a(x^2 - 7x - 18) = ax^2 - 7ax - 18a$$
Comparing to $f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c$, we have $b = -7a$.

steps.title: Find the possible values of a + b
steps.content: Calculate $a + b$: $$a + b = a + (-7a) = -6a$$ Since $a$ is an integer greater than 1, $a \in \{2, 3, 4, 5, ...\}$, so $a + b \in \{-12, -18, -24, -30, ...\}$. Only choice D ($-12$) matches a possible value, occurring when $a = 2$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
